q: Most of the fluids are not ideal ones and offer some ___ to motion.
The resistance to fluid motion (or) internal friction of fluid is called ___.
Viscosity arises when there is ___ motion between layers of the fluid.
A fluid with large viscosity resists motion because its strong ___ forces give it a lot of internal friction.
The viscosity of liquids decreases rapidly with an increase in ___.
The viscosity of gases increases with an increase in ___.
Viscosity does not change as the amount of matter changes, therefore it is an ___ property.
The SI unit of viscosity is ___ second ($\text{Pa}\cdot\text{s}$).
Pascal second is equivalent to ___ second per metre squared ($\text{N}\cdot\text{s}\cdot\text{m}^{-2}$).
The SI unit of viscosity is sometimes referred to as the ___ ($\text{Pl}$).
One way is to measure fluid's resistance to flow when an external force is applied. This is known as ___ viscosity.
The other way is to measure the resistive flow of a fluid under the weight of gravity. It is known as ___ viscosity.
Newton's law of viscosity states that the shear stress between the two adjacent layers of fluid is directly proportional to the negative value of the ___ gradient.
Shear stress is the shearing force per unit ___.
Viscosity is defined as the ratio of shearing stress to the ___ gradient.
In symbols, shear stress $\tau \propto \frac{dv}{dy}$ (or) $\tau = -\eta ___$.
In the equation $\tau = -\eta \frac{dv}{dy}$, $\eta$ is the viscosity (___ of viscosity).
Shear stress is the shearing force per unit _.
In the formulation of shear stress, the expression $\frac{dv}{dy}$ is the _.
Viscosity is defined as the ratio of shearing stress to the _.
Stokes' law states that the drag force $F$ acting upward in resistance to the fall of a spherical object in a viscous liquid is $F = $ _.
A simple way of measuring viscosity is that a small sphere ball is dropped through the fluid and time of fall and _ of fall are measured.
From the distance and time of fall, the _ can be determined.
The upward thrust acting on a sphere ball inside a fluid is given verbatim by $F_1 = $ _.
At a constant velocity of fall (terminal velocity), the upward and downward forces are in _.
The resistance to fluid motion (or) internal resistance of fluid is called _.
